title: Listening - Part 1
description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new city museum dedicated to local history.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I finally visited the 'Old-Town' museum. The recreation of the victorian street was incredibly detailed. || Woman: [1]I found the way they used wax figures a bit unsettling[/1], to be honest. They looked a bit too much like real people frozen in time. || Man: I see what you mean. It was a bit 'uncanny valley'. || Woman: [2]What I really appreciated, though, was the way they’ve integrated local stories into the larger historical events[/2]. It made it feel much more personal. || Man: I agree. It wasn't just dates and battles; it was about the actual people who lived here. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a great way to engage kids in history, as long as they aren't spooked by the shopkeeper in the corner.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I finally visited the 'Old-Town' museum. The recreation of the victorian street was incredibly detailed.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the way they used wax figures a bit unsettling, to be honest. They looked a bit too much like real people frozen in time.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see what you mean. It was a bit 'uncanny valley'.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: What I really appreciated, though, was the way they’ve integrated local stories into the larger historical events. It made it feel much more personal.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It wasn't just dates and battles; it was about the actual people who lived here.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a great way to engage kids in history, as long as they aren't spooked by the shopkeeper in the corner.

questions.question: The woman expresses some discomfort regarding

options.A: the dark lighting in the exhibits.
options.C: the small size of the museum.

questions.explanation: She finds the wax figures 'unsettling' because they look 'too much like real people' [1].

questions.question: Both speakers liked the museum's focus on
questions.explanation: They both appreciate the 'local stories' and that it was 'about the actual people' [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a new digital security policy at their company.
extracts.audio_script: Man: So, we have to change our passwords every thirty days now. It seems a bit excessive, doesn't it? || Woman: [3]I suspect the IT department is just following some generic industry standard rather than assessing our actual risks[/3]. || Man: It is a bit of a hassle. I’m running out of creative names for my dog. || Woman: [4]I’ve noticed that people are already starting to write their passwords on post-it notes[/4] because they can't remember them, which actually makes things less secure. || Man: I agree. It’s a classic case of security measures backfiring. || Woman: Exactly. If they want us to be safe, they should just provide us with a proper password manager instead of making us memorise random strings of numbers.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So, we have to change our passwords every thirty days now. It seems a bit excessive, doesn't it?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect the IT department is just following some generic industry standard rather than assessing our actual risks.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It is a bit of a hassle. I’m running out of creative names for my dog.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve noticed that people are already starting to write their passwords on post-it notes because they can't remember them, which actually makes things less secure.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s a classic case of security measures backfiring.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. If they want us to be safe, they should just provide us with a proper password manager instead of making us memorise random strings of numbers.

questions.question: The woman believes the new policy was
questions.explanation: She thinks IT is just following a 'generic standard' without 'assessing actual risks' [3].

questions.question: What unintended consequence of the policy has the woman noticed?

options.A: a decrease in the speed of logging in
options.B: a rise in risky storage practices

questions.explanation: She notes people are writing passwords on 'post-it notes' to remember them [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a new local bakery specializing in artisan donuts.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I finally tried the 'Glaze-Gallery' yesterday. That lavender and lemon donut was a revelation! || Woman: [5]I found the queuing system to be a bit chaotic[/5], to be honest. There was no clear line and the staff didn't seem to know who was next. || Man: I agree, it’s a bit of a victim of its own success. || Woman: [6]I did appreciate the quality of the ingredients, though[/6]. You can really tell they use fresh fruit instead of those artificial syrups. || Man: I agree. It makes a huge difference to the flavour. || Woman: Definitely. I’ll just make sure to go on a weekday morning next time to avoid the weekend madness.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I finally tried the 'Glaze-Gallery' yesterday. That lavender and lemon donut was a revelation!

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the queuing system to be a bit chaotic, to be honest. There was no clear line and the staff didn't seem to know who was next.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree, it’s a bit of a victim of its own success.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did appreciate the quality of the ingredients, though. You can really tell they use fresh fruit instead of those artificial syrups.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It makes a huge difference to the flavour.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Definitely. I’ll just make sure to go on a weekday morning next time to avoid the weekend madness.

questions.question: What did the woman find annoying about her visit to the bakery?

options.A: the high price of the individual items
options.B: the lack of organization in the shop
options.C: the limited range of flavors available

questions.explanation: She finds the 'queuing system' chaotic with no clear line [5].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the bakery's
questions.explanation: They both praise the 'quality of the ingredients' and the use of 'fresh fruit' [6].
